purpose: The investor map as a policy bulletin: for each of the 12 sectors the Indian government is incentivizing, where the world's companies in that sector actually are (the courting map -- 19,795 companies across 107 countries from the global classification universe, coverage-first with NO profitability gate), how many India already lists, which incentive lane courts them, and the verified foreign-investor x initiative pairings. Full data + reproducible scripts live in the companion repo focus-sector-investor-map.
method: Base = Damodaran's 48,156-company global classification (all exchanges), mapped to the 12 focus sectors -- deliberately ungated: the question is where suitable companies EXIST, not which pass a screen. Market-layer (liquidity/momentum, 20 markets) and per-name profitability (yfinance-verified, 147 names) are attribute layers in the companion repo. Initiative lanes from the site-verified ministry catalog; pairings PRID-cited from the PIB register.

pairings.method_note: Verified pairs come from PIB releases, Cabinet approvals and ministry unit-lists collected by the trade-policy program (india-trade-sector-policy-recommendations); prospective pairs name the Cabinet-approved lane and the company's publicly announced India intent. The negative findings (POSCO EC absence, Hyundai ACC lapse, White Goods attrition) are retained deliberately -- pairing quality includes knowing which pairings are stalled.

readings: The courting pools match the FDI-sourcing strategies almost one-for-one: Taiwan (789) + South Korea (487) + China (837) hold the electronics-component pool ECMS/Semicon 2.0 targets; Canada (833) and Australia (515) hold the metals/critical-minerals pool the NCMM chain needs; the US holds pharma (724) and medtech (204); Vietnam (26) and Indonesia (27) appear as shipbuilding peers -- competitors as much as sources.

India already lists meaningful domestic pools in textiles (399), chemicals (481), food (220) and steel/metals (236) -- sectors where the play is upgrading listed incumbents, versus electronics/medtech where the pool must be imported (India lists just 10 screened electronics names vs Taiwan's 789 classified).

Chemicals & Plastics is the standout asymmetry: 2,453 companies globally, 481 in India, the largest uncovered import heads, and no dedicated scheme -- the first-mover lane this program's action items have flagged since the gap analysis.

Coverage-first matters: gating on profitability first would have hidden exactly the turnaround/expansion candidates incentives exist to attract; profitability is an attribute to check per courting conversation (147 names already verified in the companion repo).
